name: Artifact And Memory Publishing Pipeline
description: Content workflow that combines assessment, human review, artifacts, and memory.
outcome: Draft content is fact-checked, approved by a human, and then finalized for publication.

definition.process_instructions: The research lead drafts the content and supporting evidence. The fact-check assessor evaluates the draft. A human approval gate decides whether the assessed draft can proceed. Once both the assessment and the human decision are clear, the publication editor finalizes the release packet.

parameters.name: goal
parameters.description: What this live workflow should accomplish

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: draft-package
stages.goal: The draft content and evidence are ready.

stages.name: operator-approval
stages.goal: A human decision exists for the assessed draft.
